title: Nearly 9,000 customers without power across Central NY after storms with high winds
summary: Central New York experienced widespread power outages affecting nearly 9,000 customers following high-wind storms on June 18, 2026. By Saturday morning, National Grid and NYSEG had restored service to most areas, with only 317 customers remaining without power. This incident reflects infrastructure resilience challenges during severe weather events relevant to the CIO's emergency management and utility coordination portfolio.

entity_refs.name: New York State Electric and Gas

political_signal.description: Power restoration speed demonstrates state utility coordination effectiveness; minimal ongoing impact suggests adequate emergency response infrastructure.

processing_notes: Article focuses on utility response rather than municipal government action; no Syracuse city department involvement detected
High resolution speed (9000→317 customers in <24hrs) suggests established coordination protocols
